title: Foundations of Health Informatics
description: An introductory graduate course covering core concepts of health informatics including clinical data standards (HL7, FHIR), electronic health record systems, interoperability frameworks, and the role of information technology in modern healthcare delivery.

title: Clinical Natural Language Processing
description: Advanced methods for extracting structured knowledge from clinical text. Topics include named entity recognition, relation extraction, de-identification, transformer-based models (BERT, ClinicalBERT), and evaluation on benchmark clinical NLP corpora.

title: Introduction to Biomedical Data Science
description: A hands-on introduction to data science techniques applied to biomedical problems. Students learn Python-based data wrangling, visualization, and machine learning using real-world clinical and genomic datasets.

title: Data Provenance and Reproducibility in Health AI
description: Examines provenance frameworks, lineage tracking, and reproducibility standards for AI systems deployed in clinical settings. Covers W3C PROV, audit logging, explainability requirements, and regulatory considerations for health AI.

title: Health Data Management
description: Principles of managing health data throughout its lifecycle. Topics include database design for clinical systems, data quality assessment, privacy and HIPAA compliance, data governance policies, and practical SQL skills for querying clinical datasets.

title: Advanced Topics: Federated Learning in Healthcare
description: A seminar-style course on privacy-preserving machine learning for healthcare. Covers federated learning architectures, differential privacy, secure aggregation, and practical challenges of multi-site clinical model training across institutional boundaries.
